summary: In this second conversation with Krishna Sharma on This or That with Bhartendra, we go deep on the one principle most investors ignore: economic equilibrium. Krishna explains why what's happening right now doesn't matter as much as what must happen next.

seo.title: Gold इतना महंगा है, फिर भी खरीदना सही है?
seo.description: In this second conversation with Krishna Sharma on This or That with Bhartendra, we go deep on the one principle most investors ignore: economic

takeaways: The core lens is economic equilibrium: what is happening right now matters less than what must happen next, which is why two years of flat markets is framed as Newton's Third Law quietly at work.
The same valuation number can mean completely different things in different moments, so comparing today to COVID on the headline figure alone will mislead you.

Asking whether to follow FIIs or retail investors is the wrong question; the episode instead hands you a Sensex 17,000 homework exercise to test whether the active funds you pay for actually earn their fees.
Gold's character has shifted from insurance to a trade asset, which is why this war pushed gold down for the first time rather than up.
Match your money to when you will spend it: liquid, short term, medium, hybrid, then equity, and only consider global investing once you have built a solid Indian portfolio of roughly ₹2 to 4 crore.
